**Defining the Scope**

I'm currently focused on defining the exact scope. The user needs a React application mimicking a provided UI design for an "Odometer Verification" system, LogiTrack. I'm carefully reviewing the reference HTML snippet and image description to identify all required components and functionalities for the UI. My goal is to create a component architecture to map the UI designs provided to React components.


**Clarifying the Components**

I've been drilling down into the specific components. I see a clear layout: a sidebar, header, and main content. The sidebar includes navigation and a user profile; the header has breadcrumbs and a notification bell. The main content will contain the title, description, and status indicator, plus a two-column card. On the left, it'll have an image with controls. The right side is the mileage input form.


**Detailing the Form Elements**

I'm now zeroing in on the form elements and validation results. There's a vehicle selector, mileage input field (manual), and an error message ("Valor diverge..."). I also need a "Reprocessar Validação" button. Below, I'm working on the "Validation Result" section with the warning alert ("Divergência Detectada"), the comparison values (user input vs. AI), and actions like "Marcar para Revisão" (red) and "Aceitar Valor da IA".


**Outlining the Architecture**

I've decided to adopt `lucide-react` for icons, it feels cleaner. I'm structuring the app with `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and component folders. I'll include `Sidebar.tsx`, `Header.tsx`, `OdometerVerification.tsx`, `StatusBadge.tsx`, and `types.ts` for data interfaces. I'm focusing on defining component-specific states for manual mileage, AI status (with loading), and vehicle selection using mock data.


**Visualizing Iconography**

I am now focusing on integrating the icons, selecting `lucide-react` for its clarity and flexibility. I'm mapping dashboard to `LayoutDashboard`, Frota to `Truck`, and so on. Also, I have decided to define specific colors, such as `#137fec`, in the Tailwind config to ensure consistency. I am trying to use Tailwind to be simple, but still allow for customization, by adding a custom color.


**Implementing the Layout**

I am now focusing on the grid layout, deciding on `grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-2`. I'm working on the image overlay logic (absolute positioning) and comparison section logic, specifically the status indicator.



**Refining the Structure**

Now I'm focusing on the component file structure and confirming the details. I've decided to use `lucide-react`, though the original HTML referenced material-icons. I'm ensuring semantically equivalent icons are used. I will structure the React application into index.html, index.tsx, App.tsx, and then component files such as Sidebar.tsx, Header.tsx, and VerificationCard.tsx. A main type file is also planned to be included. I am working on using React 18 for this project.

1. Ao receber um conjunto de imagens relacionados ao uma entrega, uma modelo de IA ou

a. Classificar se a imagem é um canhoto OU não. Ou seja, uma folha de papel. Se
puder classificar em mais opções, é um diferencial, mas não é critério. Exemplo,
nesta imagem é “Produtos”, “Caixas”, etc..
b. Apenas nas imagens que forem classificadas como “CANHOTO”, precisam
identificar mais algumas informações, que são os itens abaixo, marcados como
“CANHOTO”. Obs.: Talvez possa ser outro modelo de IA ou o mesmo. Isso
